expressions.korean: 돈을 빌리다
expressions.english: to borrow money

expressions.korean: 책을 빌리다
expressions.english: to borrow a book

expressions.korean: 힘을 빌리다
expressions.english: to get (someone's) help

expressions.korean: 돈 좀 빌릴 수 있어요?
expressions.english: Can I borrow some money?

expressions.korean: 펜 하나만 빌려도 될까요?
expressions.english: Can I borrow a pen?
expressions.notes:
펜 = pen
-이/이/여도 될까요? = Can I/we + V?

expressions.korean: 만원만 빌릴 수 있을까요?
expressions.english: Do you think I could borrow 10,000 won?

expressions.korean: 어제 빌린 돈 언제 갚을 거예요?
expressions.english: When are you going to pay me back the money that you borrowed yesterday?
expressions.notes:
언제 = when
갚다 = to pay back

expressions.korean: 도서관에 가서 책 좀 빌려 올게요.
expressions.english: I will go borrow some books from the library.
expressions.notes:
도서관 = library
-이/이/여 오다 = to go + V + and come back
-(으)르 게요, = I promise I will + V.; I will + V.

expressions.korean: 친구한테 빌린 책을 잃어버렸어요.
expressions.english: I lost the book that I borrowed from a friend.

expressions.korean: 잠깐만 핸드폰 좀 빌릴 수 있을까요?
expressions.english: Can I borrow your cellphone for a minute?
expressions.notes: 잠깐 = short time; for a moment

expressions.korean: 그 사람은 돈을 빌리고 잘 안 갚아요.
expressions.english: He usually doesn't pay back the money he owes.

expressions.korean: 이번 주말에 차 좀 빌릴 수 있을까요?
expressions.english: Can I borrow your car this weekend?

expressions.korean: 지난 일요일에는 DVD를 빌려 봤어요.
expressions.english: Last Sunday, I rented and watched some DVDs.

expressions.korean: 저번에 빌려 간 책은 언제 돌려주실 거 예요?
expressions.english: When are you going to return the book that you borrowed last time?
expressions.notes:
저번에 = the other day; last time
돌려주다 = to return something to someone, give something back

expressions.korean: 지난번에 저한테서 만 원 빌린 거 있었어요?
expressions.english: Did you forget that you borrowed 10,000 won from me last time?

expressions.korean: 밖이 너무 추워서 친구의 자켓을 빌려 입었어요.
expressions.english: It was too cold outside, so I borrowed my friend's jacket.
expressions.notes:
밖 = outside
춥다 = (place) to be cold; to feel cold
자켓 = jacket
입다 = to wear (clothes)

expressions.korean: 친구가 돈을 빌리고 한 달이 넘도록 갚지 않아요.
expressions.english: My friend borrowed some money and she hasn't paid me back for over a month.
expressions.notes:
한 달 = one month
넘다 = to be over + time/amount
-도록 = till, until, to the point where

expressions.korean: 오랜만에 만화책방에 가서 만화책을 빌려 봤어요.
expressions.english: I went to a comic book rental shop after a long time and read some comic books.
expressions.notes:
오랜만에 = after a long time
만화책방 = comic book rental shop
만화책 = comic book

expressions.korean: 도서관에서 책을 빌렸는데 반납하는 걸 깜빡했어요.
expressions.english: I borrowed some books from the library, but I forgot to return (them).
expressions.notes:
반납하다 = to return something that one rented
깜빡하다 = to completely forget (for a short period of time)

expressions.korean: 친구가 노트북을 빌려 갔다가 돌려줬는데 고장이 났어요.
expressions.english: My friend borrowed my laptop computer and gave it back, but it is not working.
expressions.notes:
-았/었/였 + and then
고장이 나다 = to break; to be broken
